title: Fermented Dense Jamaican Coconut Stew
description: A thick, savory, fermented coconut stew thats perfect for a hangover cure, packed with sweet potatoes, plantains, and a hearty dose of desiccated coconut.
instructions: Soak the desiccated coconut in the fermented coconut water for at least 4 hours or overnight; drain well before use.
Heat a splash of oil in a large pot and sauté the onion‑garlic‑ginger mix, the Scotch bonnet pepper, and dried thyme until fragrant, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced sweet potato and plantain, cooking for an additional 3 minutes while stirring.
Pour in the coconut milk, vegetable stock, fermented soy sauce, and the soaked coconut; bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat, cover, and simmer for 25 minutes until the sweet potato and plantain are tender and the stew has thickened.
Stir in lime juice, season with salt and black pepper to taste, and remove from heat.
Serve hot, spooning the dense stew into bowls; garnish with chopped cilantro if desired.
